metadata.description:
Inspired by the Many-Worlds Interpretation of quantum mechanics. 
Your minted artwork will transform based on your decisions on how to view it.
There are no rarity attributes assigned to this artwork, its value is intended to be determined by viewer aesthetic alone.

This artwork was created using p5js.

metadata.name: Southern Gothic #236
metadata.description:
“Southern Gothic” is my debut release on the fxhash platform. The project is created entirely through code. All copyrights are released to the holder of each image output, even for commercial purposes.

“Southern Gothic” borrows its name from the literary genre made famous by writers like Flannery O’Connor and Carson McCullers. Rather than illustrating a singular setting, the code generates an entire rural townscape, thereby, blurring the boundaries between representation and narrative. The individual scenes are framed frontally with an intentionally flat aesthetic character, referencing traditional folk art of the American South. The absence of human figures within these scenes adds to their overwhelming sense of darkness and forlorn. Observing multiple scenes side-by-side, one may even get the strange feeling that something terrible has happened (or is about to happen) in this rural landscape.

Pressing the down arrow saves the coded output as a .png image.

metadata.name: simple things #68
metadata.description:
There’s beauty in simple things. Sunlight. A reflection on a puddle. The flight of a bird. Observing the clouds. Spending time with loved ones.

To celebrate the re-opening of fxhash, I'd like to contribute with some simple thing to enjoy among friends who rejoin after some time.

Simple things is a piece that is just that: simple things. Nothing fancy here. Simple circles doing simple movement, with simple colours. Maybe the viewer will rejoice in observing them transit on the screen.

It has some controls: you can press “s” to save a png file. You can set a higher resolution pressing “d” followed by 1-9 to increase pixel density (if you get a black image, press “d 0” and start again with a lower number). Simple controls.

It has also colour controls: press z for brightness, x for contrast, g for gamma, b for blur, n for noise followed by + or - to alter their values. You might get some simple variations that you might enjoy. Press a to enable/disable all colour controls (except the noise).

The pieces exhibit simple variations: palettes, circle sizes, density of elements, backgrounds. The colours might be inverted sometimes. The direction of the movement changes also in some pieces. Some are grayscale pieces.

Just like in life, enjoying simple things is sometimes enough to leave behind the noise and our worries. Also like in life, if one wants something extra, they may get it by looking for it...

Artwork by Marcelo Soria-Rodríguez, @msoriaro.
Made in p5.js, and glsl for the image controls.

metadata.name: Illuminations #359
metadata.description:
Created for the FxHash Turns One week - Thank you FxHash for all the opportunities you have given generative artists and collectors, and thank you to everyone who makes, and collects, the great art here. Here are a few celebratory generative effects which come from my 2022 experiments on random walks through color and space.

There are 5 different burst patterns (Whirligig, Sky Ripple, Confetti, Classic, Sparkler) and the Cocktail type brings them all together (somewhat chaotically)

To continue with the display after it stops press c

To switch to tranquil mode press t - You will want to try this out when you have a minute

To switch back to live mode press v

To stop the display process press x

To save a 2800x2800 jpg press s

Made with p5.js - all proceeds and royalties assigned to the Processing Foundation (who also deserve a big thank you)

This work is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International License.

metadata.name: STRUCTURES #248
metadata.description:
STRUCTURES

What color do things look when you're happy?

First of all I want to dedicate this collection to all the people who paint their emotions with color, who through a chromatic range, are capable of feeling a universe of sensations most of the time inexplicable.....

 I am fascinated by how generative art creates a perfect symbiosis between art and technology, allowing through a set of algorithmic rules to give rise to compositions where shapes and colors coexist in balance, harmonized with each other that give rise to new creations.

This collection is designed to be self-contained and limited, recreating the view from different angles and phases of the day that can lead to disparate interpretations of a piece.

My work as a visual artist explores elements such as randomness and chance of linear formations and geometric gradients that broaden the spectrum to increasingly new concepts that I definitely want to explore.

I continually wonder what the geometric patterns that will emerge from my sensibility will be like and will give rise to new collections like this one, which is why I continue to investigate techniques and shapes while having fun.

"A painting is not the image of an experience, but it is the experience." Mark Rothk

metadata.name: Tangentum #233
metadata.description:
We live in a multiverse: an infinite-dimensional space of variants. Everything exists here. Everything is possible. Each subset of a multiverse has its own unique laws of physics, a set of rules, and its own distinct features.

Tangentum creates a projection of a random multiverse subset to a 2-dimensional plane. Each variation is a unique representation of the force field, that exists somewhere at some point in time.

This work starts a series of my visual exploration of functions, continuity, and boundedness, and I would love to share it with you.


Please use fullscreen or download artwork in high resolution for a better viewing experience.

Tested with Chrome 107, Safari, and Firefox. Chrome is recommended, other browsers are slower on this artwork.

Saving with high and ultra-high resolution may take a while, please be patient.


Made with love and p5js


Controls in fullscreen:
[1] - save PNG 2700 x 1800
[2] - save PNG 5400 x 3800
[3] - save PNG 10800 x 7600
[4] - save PNG 16200 x 11400

metadata.name: Nanopanorama #69
metadata.description:
Distorted noise with weird textures and funky shapes rendered using my rayhatcher algorithm.

4671 characters of pure Javascript, no libraries.

Some options can be changed via the URL:

&lw=.25 : The linewidth in millimetres.
&d=.4 : Line density (multiplier to linewidth).
&h=235 : Page height in millimetres. The aspect ratio is 16:9, for a width of 420mm, suitable for A3 plotting.
&bg=1 : Set to 0 to remove background rectangle (recommended for plotting).

When plotting, it is recommended to use a path reordering / optimizing tool such as axicli or vpype to greatly reduce the plotting time.

metadata.name: n Forms #26
metadata.description:
Hand-scrawled documentation of abstract structures. A journal from another realm.

Press 's' to save an image while in full-screen mode.

If you want to increase the resolution of the saved image, add e.g. &size=2000 after the live/fullscreen view URL to render the canvas at that specific pixel width (default = browser sized). This will be dependent on your system specifications, but outputs of up to 8000px wide, or above, should be possible.

Usually loads best in Chrome

metadata.name: EDDY #267
metadata.description:
An eddy is a circular movement of water counter to a main current. It reminds me of innovations in art, like a small countercurrent to the mainstream, surfacing new techniques and aesthetics. 

I hope that EDDY will brighten your days with its playful swirl of colors, inspired by the sea, the sky, the birds, and post-impressionist paintings.

Keyboard Controls:
[F] Fill the window.
[S] Save the current view in live mode as an image (default 1125x1500px).
[1] [2] [4] [6] Change resolution, 1.5K to 9K.
[R] Reverse course.

Made with p5js and shaders.

Created by elsif, 2022.12.

metadata.name: SoulFish #105
metadata.description:
Named after the artist Wu Che-Yu, the soul fish was born in the void and crystallized from human emotions.

Fluttering silently and slowly, their bodies almost disappeared in the light of the deep sea.
The fish swims freely, and this is the happiness of the fish.
If we are not a fish, how do we know the joy of a fish?
How do we know that we do not know the joy of fish?
We will only learn about it when we come to the riverside.

Inspired by Zhuāngzǐ: "The Debate on the Joy of Fish" c. 3rd century BC

Interactivities:
> Key S: Save
> Holding mouse in different directions: Swim 
> Click and let the fish touch the Bubbles
> Key B: Toggle border
> Key P: Pause

metadata.name: How to Draw Bubbles (◕‿◕)♡ #185
metadata.description:
[The solidness or volume of the form could be obtained by using highlights on one side of each object and shading on the opposite side.]


(⊃｡•́‿•̀｡)⊃━━━━☆


With only a few colorful lines near the edges and bright accents in the oposite corners, one rounded polygon becomes a shiny and bright voluminous surface. It's easy! 

Look out for the FOMO keyboard key to unlock more variations.




Wishing you easy, light and bubbly 2023! 
(◕‿◕)♡

metadata.name: NESTED IDENTITIES #164
metadata.description:
Nested identities redact fingerprints but deeper meaning can still be found.

Nested identifies refers to the idea that a person may have multiple identities that are interconnected and interdependent. These identities may be related to a person's cultural background, ethnicity, nationality, gender, sexual orientation, social class, profession, and other factors. It is possible that a person may want to conceal or redact certain aspects of their identity in order to protect their privacy or to avoid discrimination or persecution. However, even if certain details about a person's identity are hidden or redacted, it may still be possible to infer deeper meaning or understand more about their experiences and perspectives based on the context in which they express themselves and the information that is available.


The concept of nested identities highlights the complexity and multifaceted nature of a person's identity, and how different aspects of identity can intersect and influence each other. It is important to recognize and respect the diversity of identities that people may hold, and to be mindful of the ways in which power dynamics and social context can shape how people present and express their identities. It is also important to recognize that people's identities may evolve and change over time, and that everyone has the right to self-identify and to define their own identity on their own terms.

Press p to download a high quality export.

metadata.name: Subdivision: forms and feelings #179
Transition: Omnidimensional #100
metadata.description:
This piece is a corrupted version of Transition: Multidimensional. The code is nearly identical but there are small changes in decisive places. Small numbers have become big, muted colors have become bright and if/else statements have become if statements.

The instructions for the animations have not been changed, yet they appear vastly different from Transition: Multidimensional. Use with caution. 

— Interactions —

A - Start/stop Animation A (epileptic trigger warning)

B - Start/stop Animation B (epileptic trigger warning)

H - Load in 4x resolution

S - Save as image (only in live view)

— Credits —

Made with p5.js, Typescript and Webpack.

— License —

This project is licensed under a modified version of the Climate Strike License (CSL).
